rationale: The previous attempt's bare `assert(post1_netc.my_end_point() == pre_netc.my_end_point())` failed because H1/H2 are the antecedent of the ensures' top-level `==>`, not free hypotheses. This rewrite wraps the work in `if (H1 && H2) { ... }`: inside the branch H1∧H2 is in scope, so every nested `assert` is sound, and after the branch each becomes the conditional fact `(H1 && H2) ==> X` that Verus can chain to discharge the `(H1 && H2) ==> det_receive_with_demarshal_equal(...)` ensures. A joint case-split on the (r1.0-tag, r2.0-tag) pair forces the per-run `rr is Fail/Timeout/Packet ==> ...` arms of H1 and H2 to unfold under the same path condition, closing the disjoint-trigger gap that caused z3's `unknown`.
